message:
feat(pt-core): Expand proc_parsers with comprehensive /proc parsing

Significantly expand proc_parsers.rs from stub to full implementation
with parsers for essential /proc/[pid]/* files:

Data structures:
- IoStats: /proc/[pid]/io - rchar, wchar, syscr, syscw, read_bytes,
  write_bytes, cancelled_write_bytes for I/O profiling
- SchedStats: /proc/[pid]/schedstat - cpu_time_ns, wait_time_ns,
  timeslices for scheduler analysis
- SchedInfo: /proc/[pid]/sched - voluntary/involuntary context switches,
  priority, nice value
- MemStats: /proc/[pid]/statm - size, resident, shared, text, lib, data
  pages for memory profiling
- FdInfo: File descriptor metadata - path, flags, socket info
- CgroupMembership: Cgroup v1/v2 hierarchy membership

Parsers:
- parse_io(): Parses key:value format of /proc/[pid]/io
- parse_schedstat(): Parses space-delimited /proc/[pid]/schedstat
- parse_sched(): Extracts context switch counts from /proc/[pid]/sched
- parse_statm(): Parses space-delimited memory stats
- parse_fd_info(): Reads /proc/[pid]/fd/* symlinks with socket detection
- parse_cgroup(): Parses hierarchy:controllers:path format
- parse_wchan(): Reads wait channel name
- parse_environ(): Parses null-delimited environment (with size limit)

All parsers are defensive with proper error handling - missing/unreadable
files return None rather than errors, enabling graceful degradation when
running without elevated privileges.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
